articles.title: People who are blind from birth never develop schizophrenia—what this tells us about the psychiatric condition
articles.description: In 1950, two researchers noticed something that didn't quite add up. Hector Chevigny, a writer who had lost his sight in adulthood, and psychologist Sydell Braverman were studying the psychological lives of blind people when they stumbled upon an intriguing pattern: schizophrenia, a serious mental illness affecting people across virtually every known society, appeared to be entirely absent in people who had been blind from birth.

articles.title: Blood vessels activate self-defense response that may slow atherosclerosis progression
articles.description: A study by researchers at Baylor College of Medicine and collaborating institutions reveals a previously unrecognized way blood vessels can protect themselves from damage and slow the development of atherosclerosis. The findings, published in the Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences, have implications for both vascular precision medicine and the safety of certain emerging cancer treatments.

articles.title: Meta’s historic loss in court could cost a lot more than $375 million
articles.description:
Mark Zuckerberg. | Image: Cath Virginia / The Verge, Getty Images	

New Mexico Attorney General Raúl Torrez won a historic sum of $375 million in a landmark child safety case against Meta earlier this year. But the next stage of the fight could be even more consequential for Meta and the social media industry at large.
Beginning Monday, attorneys for Meta and New Mexico will return to a Santa Fe courthouse for a three-week public nuisance trial, where they'll argue over the changes the AG wants the judge to order Meta make to Facebook, Instagram, and WhatsApp. Those changes include adding age verification for New Mexico users, prohibiting end-to-end encryption for users under 18 and capping their use to 90  …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: Spirit Airlines shuts down after Trump’s war on Iran doubled jet fuel prices
articles.description:
Spirit Airlines planes are parked on the tarmac at the Fort Lauderdale-Hollywood International Airport on May 02, 2026 in Fort Lauderdale, Florida. | Getty Images	

The ultra-low-cost air carrier Spirit Airlines shut down operations after 34 years in business and canceled all flights at 3AM ET on Saturday morning. Its website now redirects to spiritrestructuring.com, instructing fliers not to go to airports, with air traffic control records capturing controllers and pilots signing off to each other as its last flights came in for landings after the shutdown was announced.


  
 
  
 


 

 View this post on Instagram

 
 
 
 

 
 

 
 
 

 
 
 



A statement on its website said that for people who purchased tickets directly, "Refunds for tickets purchased by credit card and debit card have been issued, and will be processed by …

The Election Commission cited widespread tampering of EVMs and compromised integrity of recorded votes at multiple booths, leading to the decision for a fresh poll on May 21.

articles.title: Kerala Assembly Elections 2026: ‘No violation of rules on strong room premises in Kozhikode’
LPG gas cylinder price today after latest hike: Check revised fuel rates in New Delhi, Mumbai, Bengaluru on 3 May
articles.description: LPG commercial gas prices rose significantly, with a  ₹993 increase in May. Check revised fuel rates in New Delhi, Mumbai, Bengaluru and other cities on 3 May after latest hike.

articles.title: Spirit Airlines shuts down: What to do if you have a ticket, refund or loyalty points
articles.description: Spirit Airlines has cancelled all flights and shut down operations. Here is what passengers need to know about refunds, rebooking, loyalty points and credit card protection.

articles.title: The Love Bombing of Gloria Choi
articles.description: A dream romance ends in murder after a woman falls for a stranger. "48 Hours" correspondent Natalie Morales reports.

articles.title: Watch: What happens on counting day? | Explained
articles.description:
Voting is over. Across Tamil Nadu, Kerala, Puducherry, West Bengal and Assam, the big question now is: who wins? 
With counting of votes set to begin on May 4, here’s a closer look at how the process is done in India — step by step.

articles.title: 5 facts on Padmaja Venugopal, BJP candidate from Kerala's Thrissur who switched from Congress
articles.description: Former Congress leader Padmaja Venugopal is contesting again from the Thrissur constituency in the 2026 elections, but this time as a BJP candidate.

articles.title: Who is Rajan J Pallan, Congress candidate from Thrissur in Kerala poll race?
articles.description: In the 2026 Kerala Assembly elections, UDF's Rajan J Pallan contested from Thrissur against LDF's Alankode Leelakrishnan and NDA's Padmaja Venugopal.

articles.title: ‘Ten lifetimes won’t be enough’: TMC’s Abhishek Banerjee dares ‘bangla birodhi’ leaders to contest from Falta
articles.description: Come to Falta. Send your strongest, send one of the godfathers from Delhi. If you have got the nerve, contest in Falta, says TMC general secretary Abhishek Banerjee

topStories.title: NFL whiff leads to lifesaving diagnosis for sales manager at Kentucky Derby
topStories.description: Attending this year's Kentucky Derby meant more for thoroughbred expert Mark Toothaker, who suffered a seizure from laughing at a whiffed NFL field goal attempt that led to a lifesaving diagnosis.

topStories.title: Vehicle carrying explosives crashes into Portland athletic club, driver dead
topStories.description: A vehicle carrying explosives crashed through the front entrance of an athletic club in downtown Portland, Oregon. The driver was killed.

topStories.title: Trump says he is reviewing Iran's new offer but doubts it is 'acceptable'
topStories.description: President Donald Trump said Saturday that he was reviewing a new offer from the Iranian regime to the end the war, but offered little optimism that the proposal would succeed."I will soon be reviewing the plan that Iran has just sent to us, but can’t imagine that it would be acceptable in that they have not yet paid a big enough price for what they have done to Humanity, and the World, over the last 47 years," the president wrote in a Truth Social post.

dailyNews.title: Massive fire destroys University of South Florida laboratory building: 'Total loss'
dailyNews.description: A massive fire broke out at the University of South Florida's Marine Science Laboratory in St. Petersburg, with no injuries reported, authorities said.

dailyNews.title: "48 Hours" show schedule
dailyNews.description: True crime. Real justice. To miss it would be a crime.

dailyNews.title: Stalked woman shot at 14 times by ex-boyfriend during horrific 911 call
dailyNews.description: Gloria Choi and her friends called Lakewood, Washington, 911 four times in 48 hours to report her being stalked by an ex-boyfriend. Two days later, he ran her off the road and riddled her truck with bullets as she was on the line with a 911 dispatcher.

dailyNews.title: Trump reviews Iranian peace proposal, warns strikes could resume
dailyNews.description: US president says he is considering the 14-point peace plan, but warns strikes could resume if Tehran 'misbehaves'.

dailyNews.title: Cameraman gets caught risking it all to film World Cup's 'sexiest fan' in the Formula 1 paddock
dailyNews.description: A cameraman lost focus during Sky Sports coverage when paddock guest Ivana Knoll, the World Cup's "sexiest fan," walked by.

dailyNews.title: Massive 2,000-pound sea lion shocks tourists at iconic US hot spot: 'He’s like a Volkswagen!'
dailyNews.description: A rare Steller sea lion nicknamed Chonkers, weighing up to 2,000 pounds, has become an overnight sensation at San Francisco's popular Pier 39 waterfront.

dailyNews.title: Connor McDavid delivers message Oilers fans won’t want to hear
dailyNews.description: Connor McDavid says the Edmonton Oilers are an average team with high expectations and agrees the organization has taken a step back as a whole.

dailyNews.title: Virginia Tech speaker calls for ‘end of US empire,’ praises Oct. 7 in ‘Death to America’ remarks
dailyNews.description: Mohamed Abdou, barred from Columbia for supporting terror groups, allegedly urged students to disrupt the U.S. defense industry at a Virginia Tech event.

dailyNews.title: Iran live updates: Latest on Iran deal; US fast-tracks arms sales to Mideast partners
dailyNews.description: President Trump told Congress this week that hostilities "have terminated."

dailyNews.title: Veterans could decide the  midterms, and the VA is why
dailyNews.description: Republican candidates face growing pressure to prioritize VA reform as veterans' support could decide control of the House in the 2026 midterms.

dailyNews.title: WATCH:  America Strong: Oldest TSA officer in the US
dailyNews.description: John Wayne, 90, recounts how joining the Transportation Security Administration became a second career after financial hardship.

dailyNews.title: Germany says U.S. troop withdrawal 'anticipated', Spain and Italy could be next
dailyNews.description: Germany's defense minister is playing down the impact of the Pentagon's decision to pull 5,000 troops from the country, but the move has rattled NATO allies and added to growing fears that Europe can no longer rely on Washington.

topStories.title: Spirit Airlines shutting down after failed effort at government rescue deal
topStories.description: The budget carrier Spirit Airlines is ceasing operations after failing to land a $500 million bailout from the Trump administration.

topStories.title: Spirit Airlines shuts down after Trump’s war on Iran doubled jet fuel prices
topStories.description:
Spirit Airlines planes are parked on the tarmac at the Fort Lauderdale-Hollywood International Airport on May 02, 2026 in Fort Lauderdale, Florida. | Getty Images	

The ultra-low-cost air carrier Spirit Airlines shut down operations after 34 years in business and canceled all flights at 3AM ET on Saturday morning. Its website now redirects to spiritrestructuring.com, instructing fliers not to go to airports, with air traffic control records capturing controllers and pilots signing off to each other as its last flights came in for landings after the shutdown was announced.


  
 
  
 


 

 View this post on Instagram

 
 
 
 

 
 

 
 
 

 
 
 



A statement on its website said that for people who purchased tickets directly, "Refunds for tickets purchased by credit card and debit card have been issued, and will be processed by …
Read the full story at The Verge.

topStories.title: Spirit Airlines, low-cost innovator, shuts down after Trump bailout plan fails
topStories.description: The airline said early Saturday it had “no choice” but to wind down operations immediately, dashing hopes of a last-minute financial lifeline.

topStories.description: Trump’s combative defense secretary has grown more self-assured of his job security despite his early missteps in the role, people familiar with the matter said.

dailyNews.title: Fernando Mendoza responds to question of whether he'll visit White House
dailyNews.description: Raiders rookie Fernando Mendoza says he may skip the White House visit with Trump, citing OTAs that don't actually start until a week after May 11.

dailyNews.title: Trump’s Vision for D.C. ‘Garden of Heroes’ Statues Grows in Size and Cost
dailyNews.description: Plans for the project include formal gardens, reflecting pools and plazas alongside the statues of 250 notable Americans, according to documents obtained by The New York Times.

dailyNews.title: The Americans Who Could Be in Trump’s ‘Garden of Heroes’ Statue Park in D.C.
dailyNews.description: President Trump plans to build a park along the Potomac River featuring life-size statues of 250 Americans.

dailyNews.title: The Cannabis Industry’s New Best Friend? President Trump
dailyNews.description: The administration’s decision to relax federal regulations on medical marijuana comes with big tax breaks for many cannabis companies, and could drive new investment in the budding sector.

dailyNews.title: An Insurgency Threatens U.S. Mining Ambitions in Pakistan
dailyNews.description: Attacks by the Baloch Liberation Army could derail Pakistan’s plans for a billion-dollar mining deal with the Trump administration.

dailyNews.title: With Iran, the U.S. Stands to Lose Its Reputation, Its Friends or Its Soul
dailyNews.description: President Trump has overextended the empire dangerously.

dailyNews.title: Trump Shouldn’t Expect Much From His China Trip
dailyNews.description: China no longer looks to an erratic America as a model to emulate, but as a distraction to be managed.

dailyNews.title: Trump says he is reviewing new Iran peace proposal
dailyNews.description: President Trump told reporters Saturday he is reviewing a new 14-point peace proposal that was submitted by Iran.

dailyNews.title: Trump says U.S. Navy is "sort of like pirates" in Strait of Hormuz
dailyNews.description: President Trump compared the U.S. Navy to pirates, as it enforces the blockade of Iranian ports near the Strait of Hormuz. The U.S. also warned shipping companies that they could face penalties for paying tolls to Iran to safely pass through the critical waterway. Ramy Inocencio has more.

dailyNews.title: Rising oil prices cause political headache for Trump administration
dailyNews.description: Saturday marked 61 days since the start of the war with Iran, and gas prices continue to soar. Olivia Gazis reports on how rising prices are causing new political issues for President Trump.

dailyNews.title: Trump tells Congress hostilities in Iran 'have terminated' as war powers deadline hits
dailyNews.description: Trump said there "is tremendous discord" among Iran's leaders, which has complicated efforts to end the war that the United States began in late February.

dailyNews.title: Trump says he's raising EU auto tariffs to 25%
dailyNews.description: The Supreme Court struck down Trump's "reciprocal" tariffs earlier this year, and Europe warned that its trade deal with Washington could be in jeopardy.
